text: Last class we described a scene happening right now.
Today we describe a real game and our weekly routine.
Right now, she is playing in the final.
The team trains twice a week.
Carolina, look at the other team. They are attacking fast. We need to defend now!
I am ready, coach. I usually defend on the right, so I am moving there now.
Good. Beatriz has the ball. Pass to her and then run to the court corner.
I am passing the ball now. Look, she is open! She is going to throw it!
She scores! Great work. You always play well in the final minutes.
Thank you, coach! We are winning now, and I think we are going to win the match!
Right now we are winning the match.
My team trains twice a week.
Look, she is throwing the ball now!

Good afternoon, everyone, and welcome to the handball final here at the sports center. The two teams are walking onto the court right now, and the crowd is cheering. The match starts in just a moment. Remember, a handball team has seven players, and they play two halves of thirty minutes. And here we go! Carolina has the ball. She is running fast down the court. She passes to a teammate, she gets the ball back, and she is throwing it now. She scores! What a player! Her team is winning by two points. The coach is shouting instructions from the side. Normally this team defends very well, and right now they are defending again. The other team is trying hard, but Carolina is too quick today. The crowd is on its feet. This is a wonderful match, and there is still ten minutes to play.

Hi, this is Carolina. You asked me about handball, so here is my week. I play handball because I love it. My team trains twice a week, on Tuesdays and Thursdays, after school. On Saturdays we usually have a match. I always wake up early on game day, and I never eat too much before a game. On the court, I am normally a fast player, and I like to pass the ball and to score. Right now I am sitting at home and I am watching a video of our last match, so I can learn from it. In the video, the coach is explaining a new move, and my team is defending really well. We do not win every time, but we improve every week. Next Saturday we have an important match, and I think we are ready to win. Come and watch us play!

The two teams walk onto the court and the match starts. Carolina gets the ball and runs fast down the court. She passes to a teammate and gets the ball back. She throws the ball and scores a point. Her team defends well and wins the match.
My team trains twice a week.
Look! She is throwing the ball now.
The match starts at three o'clock.
Right now we are winning the match.
I usually pass the ball to the center.
Listen! The coach is shouting instructions.
